source.title: The temporal and spectral characteristics of expectations and prediction errors in pain and thermoception

confidence_rationale: EEG temporal/spectral characterisation of pain predictive coding. Shows alpha-to-beta activity reflects expectation, and gamma activity is negatively modulated by prediction errors. The finding that pain PE inverts the typical sensory-cortex gamma-PE signature (which normally increases with PE) suggests pain uses a distinct computational dialect even within predictive coding. Useful but not architecturally decisive -- it says 'pain PC uses different oscillatory code' rather than 'pain has N forward models'.
failure_signatures: If pain forward prediction used the same oscillatory code as visual/auditory PC, these data would show positive gamma PE modulation; they show the opposite.
Does not falsify either the shared-substrate or parallel-substrate architecture directly.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Alpha-to-beta oscillatory activity tracks stimulus intensity expectation; gamma-band activity is negatively modulated by the absolute prediction error. This pattern differs from the positive gamma-PE coupling in visual and auditory perception but resembles the gamma-for-correct pattern in working memory.
mapping.ree_translation: The pain PE signal has a distinct oscillatory fingerprint. In REE terms, this is evidence that the pain forward-prediction implementation is not just a re-parameterised visual predictive coder. It is agnostic on whether E2_harm_a and E2_harm_s share substrate, but it does say that whatever substrate is chosen, the PE computation has a pain-specific computational character.
mapping.mapping_caveat: EEG cannot distinguish A-delta from C-fiber streams. The temporal profile is averaged across both. The finding speaks to pain-vs-non-pain modality dialect, not within-pain stream architecture.
mapping.source_context: Human EEG, probabilistic heat-pain expectation paradigm.

confidence_components.notes: Mapping fidelity low because the paper does not address the shared-vs-separate question directly. Included because it tightens the biological constraints on MECH-258 and indicates pain PC has a distinct oscillatory regime.
